description: Senior Software Engineer in a fintech environment, contributing to a cloud-native payment processing platform built on a distributed microservices architecture

Core contributor to payment processing services covering terminal lifecycle management, transaction operations, payment actions (tokenization, refund), merchant hierarchy authorization, and resilience policy implementation

Built and maintained an event-driven service processing Kafka events across the full payment lifecycle — request handling, funding events, history timeline construction, currency conversion, and out-of-order event resolution
Developed a report scheduling service supporting one-off and recurring executions, with email delivery, downloadable artifact generation, and schedule lifecycle management
Contributed to the adoption of OpenTelemetry observability across multiple services — the API gateway, payment processing, event handling, and reporting
Built an internal code generation tool to auto-generate C# database objects from MongoDB JSON Schema specifications

Contributed to a distributed payment switch system — implementing payment message protocol handling improvements, transaction risk rule processing, ISO 8583 protocol message mapping, and service reliability monitoring
Maintained and evolved a shared NuGet package ecosystem used across the legacy platform, covering cryptography, caching, messaging, audit logging, system settings, and Windows service management
Worked alongside UI engineers to develop React components for the order processing portal module
Joined a new team that owned readmodel services handling customer and order information, integrated with Salesforce
Worked on multiple microservices that handled courier integration within the platform (DHL, UPS, FedEx)
Joined the API Champions group that provided guidance and validated cross-team API-first designs, maintaining close contact with the platform architecture team
Contributed to CI/CD pipelines for new microservices created by the team (Jenkins Declarative Pipelines)
Contributed to internal cross-company frameworks
Mentored and onboarded team newcomers
Continued on the same team from the Plug-in Programme, taking on expanded responsibilities
Took on increased responsibilities within the team, handling more tasks and was also assigned to manage releases
Was later added to the on-call rotation, handling and documenting production incidents
Contributed to a portal module owned by the team, featuring dashboards that surfaced merchant KPIs
Selected for the Plug-in Programme through multiple interview rounds, targeting recently graduated students
Had intensive onboarding weeks to introduce new joiners to tools and industry best practices
Joined a team that developed a portal module focused on merchant order processing
Final-year Bachelor's internship as part of the Informatics Engineering degree programme
Built an enterprise expenses app using PowerApps
Built an expenses reporting backoffice with categorization and manager approval connected to the app
